label: plain_text
text:
Once when Srinivasa Ramanujan was working with
G. H. Hardy at the University of Cambridge, Hardy had
         s
ill. Hardy had ridden in a taxicab numbered 1729 and
he remarked that 1729 was ‘rather a dull number,
adding that he hoped that this was not a bad sign.
Ramanujan immediately replied, “No, Hardy, it is a
very interesting number. It is the smallest number
that can be expressed as the sum of two cubes in two
different ways".
